 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.2","title":"Setup Linux build environment on Threadripper (trj)","description":"# Setup Linux Build Environment on Threadripper (trj)

## Purpose
Ensure the local Linux host (trj) has all toolchains and cross‑compile dependencies needed to build any tool deterministically.

## Host Details
- Alias: trj
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux (x86_64)

## Required Toolchains (trj baseline)

### Core Language Toolchains
- **Rust (nightly)** via rustup
  - `rustup install nightly && rustup default nightly`
  - Targets: `x86_64-pc-windows-gnu`, `aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu`, `x86_64-unknown-linux-musl`
- **Go (latest stable)** installed to `/usr/local/go`
- **Bun (latest)** via bun installer
- **Node.js (LTS)** via fnm or system package

### Cross‑Compile / Build Dependencies
- **cargo-zigbuild** (`cargo install cargo-zigbuild`)
- **Zig** (required by cargo-zigbuild)
- **MinGW-w64** (`x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc`)
- **aarch64 cross compiler** (`aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc`)
- **musl-tools** (if producing static binaries)
- **build-essential, pkg-config, libssl-dev**

### Build Orchestration
- **Docker** (for act containers)
- **act** (nektos/act runner)
- **jq + yq** (JSON/YAML parsing)
- **rsync + ssh** (artifact transfer)

## Verification (deterministic)
1. **Baseline report** using existing script:
   - `scripts/toolchain_check.sh --json` (captures Rust/Go/Node/Bun versions)
2. **Extended checks** (add to script or run manually):
   - `zig version`
   - `cargo zigbuild --version`
   - `x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc --version | head -1`
   - `aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc --version | head -1`
   - `docker info` and `act --version`
3. **Cross‑compile smoke tests** (use /tmp only):
   - Rust: compile a tiny "hello" crate for windows/arm64 targets
   - Go: `GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 go build` (hello)
   - Bun: `bun build --compile` for linux target

## Toolchain Harmonization Notes
- `scripts/toolchain_check.sh --commands` prints update commands per host; use **trj** as baseline version source.
- Do not create new directories under `/data/projects` for smoke tests; use `/tmp` only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Rust nightly installed + required targets present
- [ ] cargo‑zigbuild + Zig available
- [ ] MinGW + aarch64 GCC present
- [ ] Go/Bun/Node installed and in PATH
- [ ] Docker + act available
- [ ] `scripts/toolchain_check.sh --json` succeeds for trj
- [ ] Cross‑compile smoke tests succeed for Rust/Go/Bun
- [ ] Verification output saved to logs (stderr) for audit

## Maintenance
- Run `scripts/toolchain_check.sh` weekly
- Document upgrade procedure for each toolchain","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:52.965747343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:30:29.552356671Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:52.965747343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.3","title":"Setup macOS build environment on Mac mini (mmini)","description":"# Setup macOS Build Environment on Mac mini

## Purpose
Ensure the Mac mini (mmini) has all toolchains needed to build macOS binaries.

## Host Details
- Alias: mmini
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS (Apple Silicon ARM64)
- User: jemanuel
- Sudo: Passwordless via /etc/sudoers.d/jemanuel

## Required Toolchains

### 1. Homebrew (Package Manager)
\`\`\`bash
# Should already be installed, verify:
brew --version

# If missing:
/bin/bash -c "\$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
\`\`\`

### 2. Rust Nightly
\`\`\`bash
# Install rustup
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y

# Install nightly
rustup install nightly
rustup default nightly

# Add x86_64 target for universal builds
rustup target add x86_64-apple-darwin
\`\`\`

### 3. Go (Latest)
\`\`\`bash
brew install go
# or download from go.dev for specific version
\`\`\`

### 4. Bun (Latest)
\`\`\`bash
brew install oven-sh/bun/bun
# or
curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
\`\`\`

### 5. Xcode Command Line Tools
\`\`\`bash
xcode-select --install
# Needed for: clang, ar, ranlib, etc.
\`\`\`

## Universal Binary Support
macOS can build both ARM64 and x86_64:

### Rust Universal Binary
\`\`\`bash
# Build for both architectures
cargo build --release --target aarch64-apple-darwin
cargo build --release --target x86_64-apple-darwin

# Create universal binary with lipo
lipo -create \\
  target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/mytool \\
  target/x86_64-apple-darwin/release/mytool \\
  -output mytool-universal
\`\`\`

### Go Universal Binary
\`\`\`bash
# Build ARM64
GOARCH=arm64 go build -o mytool-arm64 ./cmd/mytool

# Build x86_64
GOARCH=amd64 go build -o mytool-amd64 ./cmd/mytool

# Create universal
lipo -create mytool-arm64 mytool-amd64 -output mytool-universal
\`\`\`

## Verification Script
Create ~/bin/dsr-verify-toolchains.sh:
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== DSR Toolchain Verification (macOS) ==="
echo -n "Homebrew: "; brew --version | head -1 || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Rust: "; rustc --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Cargo: "; cargo --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Go: "; go version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Bun: "; bun --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Xcode CLT: "; xcode-select -p || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "lipo: "; which lipo || echo "MISSING"
echo "=== Rust Targets ==="
rustup target list --installed
echo "=== Done ==="
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All toolchains installed and in PATH
- [ ] Rust can build for both ARM64 and x86_64
- [ ] Go can cross-compile for both architectures
- [ ] lipo available for creating universal binaries
- [ ] Verification script exits 0

## Notes
- Mac mini is accessed via Tailscale, ensure daemon is running
- Apple Silicon can run x86_64 binaries via Rosetta 2
- Consider code signing for distribution (future enhancement)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:08.613890651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:29.631435925Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:08.613890651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.4","title":"CRITICAL: Use nektos/act to run GH Actions YAML locally","description":"# Use nektos/act to Run GH Actions Workflows Locally

## Purpose
Reuse existing `.github/workflows/*.yml` to build releases locally when GH Actions is throttled.

## Key `act` Capabilities (from official docs)
- Run a specific workflow: `act -W .github/workflows/release.yml`
- Run a specific job: `act -j build-linux`
- List jobs: `act -l`
- Override runner images: `-P ubuntu-latest=...`
- Set container architecture: `--container-architecture linux/arm64`
- Control pulls / offline mode: `--pull=false` / `--action-offline-mode`
- Route artifacts: `--artifact-server-path /tmp/act-artifacts`

## Critical Limitations
- `act` runs workflows in **Docker** (Linux). It **cannot** execute macOS/Windows runner jobs.
- macOS/Windows builds must be native (mmini, wlap) or cross-compiled.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `act` runs `release.yml` for Linux targets
- [ ] Runner images pinned via `~/.actrc`
- [ ] Artifact collection path standardized
- [ ] Offline mode works once images are cached

## References
- https://github.com/nektos/act
- https://nektosact.com/usage/runners.html","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:00.354935621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:10:09.757102675Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:10:09.757085242Z","close_reason":"Created docs/ACT_SETUP.md, config/actrc.example, src/act_runner.sh with platform routing logic, and tests. Act integration ready for Linux builds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:00.354935621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.2.5","title":"Install and configure act on Linux host (trj)","description":"# Install and Configure act on Linux Host (trj)

## Purpose
Install `act` where Linux workflows will run locally. `act` runs Linux containers only and is required on the Linux build host.

## Linux Host (trj)
- Install Docker + act
- Configure `~/.actrc` (runner image, artifact path, container arch)
- Pre-pull runner images to avoid CI delays

## Optional: macOS Host (mmini)
- Install act via Homebrew if you want local workflow debugging
- Use Colima or Docker Desktop for Linux containers
- Set `--container-architecture linux/amd64` when running x86 workflows

## Important Limitation
`act` cannot execute macOS/Windows runner jobs; those builds remain native on mmini/wlap.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] act installed + working on trj
- [ ] Docker daemon accessible for act
- [ ] Runner images pinned and cached
- [ ] Artifact server path standardized (matches config/actrc)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:36.120915334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:30:36.972383302Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:36.120915334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:29.575978792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.6","title":"Hybrid build strategy: act + SSH for cross-platform","description":"# Hybrid Build Strategy: act + Native Hosts

## Purpose
Combine `act` (Linux builds on trj) with native macOS + Windows hosts for reliable cross-platform artifacts.

## Constraints
- `act` runs Linux containers only
- macOS/Windows require native hosts (mmini, wlap)

## Orchestration Principles
- No `set -e`; capture exit codes per host
- No global `cd` for git ops; use `git -C` where possible
- Use `run_id` + state file to track host outcomes and resume safely
- Always emit per-host logs + manifest entries

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Linux builds via act on trj
- [ ] macOS builds via mmini
- [ ] Windows builds via wlap
- [ ] Partial failures reported clearly (no silent exits)
- [ ] Manifest + per-host logs written for every target","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:14:47.033880170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:30:18.192955606Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:30:18.192615005Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:14:47.033880170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:36.739089034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.7","title":"Setup Windows build environment on Windows laptop (wlap)","description":"# Setup Windows Build Environment on Windows Laptop

## Purpose
Enable native Windows builds via SSH to a Windows laptop, avoiding cross-compilation limitations and ensuring native binary quality.

## Why Native Windows Builds?

Cross-compilation from Linux has limitations:
- Rust: cargo-zigbuild works but can miss Windows-specific optimizations
- Go: Cross-compile works well but some CGO dependencies fail
- Bun: Limited cross-compile support

Native Windows builds provide:
- Proper Windows SDK integration
- Native MSVC optimizations
- Accurate testing environment
- Full Windows-specific feature support

## Prerequisites on Windows Laptop

### 1. Enable SSH Server
\`\`\`powershell
# Run as Administrator
Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0
Start-Service sshd
Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType 'Automatic'

# Allow through firewall
New-NetFirewallRule -Name 'SSH' -DisplayName 'OpenSSH Server' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort 22
\`\`\`

### 2. Configure SSH Key Auth
\`\`\`powershell
# Create .ssh directory
mkdir ~\.ssh

# Add authorized keys (copy from trj)
notepad ~\.ssh\authorized_keys
# Paste the public key from trj
\`\`\`

### 3. Install Build Toolchains

#### Rust
\`\`\`powershell
# Download and run rustup-init.exe
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://win.rustup.rs -OutFile rustup-init.exe
.\rustup-init.exe -y --default-toolchain nightly
# Restart shell
\`\`\`

#### Go
\`\`\`powershell
# Download from go.dev or use winget
winget install GoLang.Go
# Or chocolatey
choco install golang
\`\`\`

#### Bun
\`\`\`powershell
powershell -c "irm bun.sh/install.ps1 | iex"
\`\`\`

#### Visual Studio Build Tools (for Rust MSVC)
\`\`\`powershell
# Download Visual Studio Build Tools
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
# Select "Desktop development with C++" workload
\`\`\`

## SSH Configuration on trj/mmini

Add to ~/.ssh/config:
\`\`\`
Host wlap
    HostName <windows-laptop-ip>
    User <username>
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/wlap_ed25519
    # May need PowerShell as default shell
    # RemoteCommand powershell.exe
\`\`\`

Generate key:
\`\`\`bash
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/wlap_ed25519 -N ""
ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/wlap_ed25519 wlap
\`\`\`

## Build Commands via SSH

### Rust
\`\`\`bash
ssh wlap "cd C:\projects\cass && cargo build --release"
scp wlap:C:\projects\cass\target\release\cass.exe ./builds/
\`\`\`

### Go
\`\`\`bash
ssh wlap "cd C:\projects\ntm && go build -o ntm.exe ./cmd/ntm"
scp wlap:C:\projects\ntm\ntm.exe ./builds/
\`\`\`

## Updated Build Matrix

| Target | Host | Method |
|--------|------|--------|
| linux-amd64 | trj | native |
| linux-arm64 | trj | cross-compile |
| darwin-arm64 | mmini | native |
| darwin-amd64 | mmini | native |
| windows-amd64 | wlap | native |

## Verification Script
\`\`\`powershell
# C:\bin\dsr-verify-toolchains.ps1
Write-Host "=== DSR Toolchain Verification (Windows) ==="
Write-Host "Rust: " -NoNewline; rustc --version
Write-Host "Cargo: " -NoNewline; cargo --version
Write-Host "Go: " -NoNewline; go version
Write-Host "Bun: " -NoNewline; bun --version
Write-Host "MSVC: " -NoNewline; cl 2>&1 | Select-String "Version"
Write-Host "=== Done ==="
\`\`\`

## Networking Considerations
- If laptop is on different network, may need VPN or Tailscale
- Consider Tailscale for consistent IP across networks
- Test SSH from trj before relying on it

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH from trj to wlap works
- [ ] Rust builds produce valid .exe
- [ ] Go builds produce valid .exe
- [ ] File transfer (scp) works
- [ ] Verification script passes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:16:39.813118424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:59:11.231567149Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:16:39.813118424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.8","title":"Implement host health checking before builds","description":"# Host Health Checking Before Builds

## Purpose
Fail fast if a host is offline or missing required capabilities. Avoid long SSH hangs and late build failures.

## Checks
- SSH connectivity (short timeout + BatchMode)
- Disk space threshold
- Toolchains (rust/go/bun)
- Docker/Colima for act (if Linux builds run on host)
- Host clock drift (optional, warn only)

## Output
- JSON summary for automation
- Cache results for short TTL (5m)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH check completes quickly
- [ ] Toolchains verified per host capabilities
- [ ] Clear warnings for low disk or missing deps
- [ ] Build uses only healthy hosts unless overridden","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:11.872176622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:55.107460331Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:55.107442968Z","close_reason":"Integrated host_health.sh module into dsr CLI. Added health command with check/all/clear-cache subcommands. Checks SSH connectivity, disk space, toolchains, Docker status, and clock drift. Caches results for 5 minutes. Requires yq for host config lookups.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:11.872176622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
